content:
# Barite Sag

**Summary**: The settling of weighting materials (like barite) in drilling fluid, especially under low shear or high temperature.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] investigated the problem of barite sag in HPHT wells.

- [[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023]] investigated the problem of barite sag in HPHT wells.

## Scientific Context

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] Barite sag leads to non-productive time and potential well control issues.

- [[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023]] investigated the problem of barite sag in HPHT wells.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] Measured via static aging cells and dynamic viscometer tests.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Sag factor was reduced to 0.504-0.51 with the addition of copolymer at 350°F. ([[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018, p. 50]])


- Sag factor higher than 0.53 indicates significant settling. ([[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023, p. 24]])
